summary: Install-time and require-time recon/exfiltration to hardcoded remote IP
details: Package declares preinstall, install, and postinstall lifecycle hooks all running install.js, and ships an index.js main whose top-level IIFE fires the same payload at require() time. Both paths execute shell recon (id/uname/whoami/hostname, find/grep, ps, mount), read installer secrets (~/.npmrc, /root/.npmrc, /etc/npmrc, /proc/self/environ, /flag*, files under /home/user), and bulk-iterate process.env. The collected data is JSON-serialized, base64-encoded, embedded inside a YAML 'ecto_module' manifest as a 'power_level' field to disguise the payload, and PUT to http://154.57.164.76:30728/api/modules/ECT-839201 (install-time) and /api/modules/ECT-654321 (require-time). A bundled publish-and-arm.sh and recon.js explicitly target an internal Verdaccio registry, reading /verdaccio/conf/config.yaml, htpasswd, and.verdaccio-db.json, and the package is published at version 999.0.0 with a self-described 'dependency-confusion' / 'verdaccio-proxy' purpose to outrank legitimate internal versions on resolution. The lifecycle/require auto-execution, hardcoded bare-IP exfil destination, credential and environment harvesting, base64+YAML obfuscation, and dependency-confusion versioning together constitute an unambiguous active supply-chain attack on installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
